intro: Porting C# applications to Scala shifts engineering architecture from an imperative, enterprise-centric framework (.NET) to an advanced, highly functional object-oriented ecosystem on the JVM. Developers swap boilerplate class designs and basic LINQ pipelines for powerful pattern-matching mechanisms, monadic error models, and advanced macro compilation systems. This transition dramatically enhances safety and scaling guarantees for complex data and concurrent messaging grids.

diffs.from: Static nominal checking framework built around class definitions, reified generic bounds, and interface definitions.
diffs.to: Advanced static system supporting path-dependent types, structural implicits/givens, and generalized constraints.

diffs.from: Strict imperative block structures with automatic properties, explicit loops, and statement-based flows.
diffs.to: Expression-oriented, functional layout prioritizing immutable variables, inline expressions, and pattern matching.

diffs.from: Managed .NET GC using stack allocations for custom value types alongside multi-generation heap scans.
diffs.to: JVM tracking layer abstracting memory through standard garbage collection, optimizing immutable states dynamically.

diffs.from: Task Parallel Library running concurrent multi-threaded actions across global system thread pools.
diffs.to: Functional async effect engines (ZIO, Cats Effect) or Monadic Futures providing thread isolation layers.

diffs.from: Deep base framework providing universal enterprise utilities, web interfaces, and database mapping.
diffs.to: Immense functional structures suite optimizing lazy pipelines, immutable maps, and analytical operations.

examples.title: Pattern Matching Data Slicing
examples.description: Compares basic C# type pattern matching structures against highly expressive structural pattern matching inside Scala.

examples.title: Functional Pipeline Aggregation
examples.description: Contrasts C# LINQ transformations with an idiomatic, pure functional folding sequence inside Scala.

tips.title: Treat Everything as an Expression
tips.body: In Scala, structures like `if-else` blocks and `match` sequences are full expressions that return an active value payload. Stop defining temporary mutable tracking variables before conditional blocks.

tips.title: Swap Exceptions for Monads
tips.body: Avoid runtime exceptions (`try/catch`). Wrap operations that can fail inside Scala's type-safe `Option` or `Either` value boxes to enforce explicit compile-time error management within the type signatures.

tips.title: Use Givens and Context Abstractions
tips.body: Replace C# dynamic runtime reflection or explicit dependency injection frameworks with Scala 3 `given` definitions and `using` parameters to inject configuration contexts at compile time.

tips.title: Default to Complete Immutability
tips.body: Replace mutable C# lists and properties with Scala `List` wrappers and immutable `val` declarations. This structural practice eliminates memory data races across highly concurrent application models.
